entry.title: Re: SQL Anywhere on Linux
entry.content: What is the sizeof a SQLWCHAR in the unixODBC headers. The SQL Anywhere SQLWCHAR type is UTF16, not UTF32. There are Linux ODBC drivers that treat SQLWCHAR as 32-bit characters. Also, you could give the SQL Anywhere ODBC driver a try, compiling your application with the SQL Anywhere ODBC headers?

entry.title: AI across Customer Project processes
entry.content:
AI embedded in Cloud ERP systems helps simplify this process. Instead of working with disconnected tools and manual tasks, teams can manage the entire customer project lifecycle in one environment. AI supports decision-making, automates routine activities, and provides insights throughout the process—from opportunity creation to project monitoring and closure. 

That’s why AI across end-to-end processes drives better results for your business. 

But what does that look like in practice? Check out this video, which walks through how SAP helps teams manage customer projects from opportunity to delivery. 

What's on this page 

  1. From Lead to Opportunity in Seconds 
  2. Automated Project Setup 
  3. AI-Powered Staffing 
  4. Track Time smarter 
  5. Intelligent Billing & Profit Clarity 
  6. Performance Monitoring & Closure 

From Lead to Opportunity in Seconds 

The process starts with identifying and qualifying the right lead. The Deal Qualification Assistant enriches lead data, highlights buying signals, and keeps the pipeline updated in the background. 

Then, based on customer activity, the AI Assistant evaluates engagement and turns the lead into an opportunity within seconds. This reduces manual work for teams, while providing more insights and freeing up time for other tasks. 

Once the opportunity is created, Guided Selling Assistant recommends the next actions, flag missing information, and keep the deal on track. 

 After customer discussions, the quotation process begins. AI helps generate proposal communication and supports bid estimate preparation based on project requirements and historical project data. The estimate is then used to build the final proposal and move the deal toward closure. 

Automated Project Setup 

After the deal is confirmed, the project needs to be created in the system. 

With the help of The Project Setup agent, the project manager can create a new project within seconds. The manager simply prompts the agent to create a project and provides basic information of the Project. The agent automatically generates the project structure using predefined best practices. 

AI-Powered Staffing  

Once the project is created, the next step is to assign the right team members. 

The Advanced Skill Match capability analyzes employee skills, experience, and availability to identify the most suitable resources for the project. The project manager can review and compare suggested candidates and select the best fit for the required roles. 

This helps ensure that the project team is composed of specialists who match the project requirements.

Track Time smarter 

Accurate time and expense tracking are essential for project transparency. 

Instead of manually checking for missing entries, the Resource Manager can ask Joule to identify employees who have not recorded their working hours. Joule quickly returns a list of missing time entries and sends reminders to the relevant employees. 

This process takes only a few seconds, helping maintain healthy profitability and prevent revenue leakage. 

Intelligent Billing & Profit Clarity 

SAP Cloud ERP delivers precise, transparent billing across the entire project lifecycle, helping businesses protect project profitability and accelerate cash flow. 

During the pre-billing phase, teams can leave Smart Notes for clients and leads, providing full context behind each billed activity and reducing time spent on clarifications. 

From there, AI supports billing calculations based on rates, resource allocation, and workload, reducing manual effort and keeping the process faster and fully transparent. At the billing-closure stage, if any issues arise, Joule Assistant quickly resolves discrepancies and ensures accurate posting, so final invoices are sent without delays and payments arrive faster. 

Performance Monitoring & Closure 

Throughout the project, the manager monitors progress, financial status, and operational activities. 

AI-supported analytics in the Project Profitability Overview help track overall performance and provide actionable insights. Once all project activities are complete and results are validated, the manager wraps up the project efficiently, with transparent results and maximized profit.

entry.title: Licence Usage Report Demo for the Compensation Module
entry.content:
Introduction

Over the past few weeks I've been getting a fair amount of questions about the Licence Usage Report that was added to the Compensation Home screen in one of the 2025 release. Here is a quick visual explanation of how it works and how things are calculated on top of the existing documentation. 

 

Recording

In this 6 minutes recording we do a demonstration where we edit different compensation and variable pay templates to see how it impacts the Licence Usage Report.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

Archiving worksheets will not lead them to drop off the count. Only inactivating a template will but we don't recommend doing it unless there is a good reason for it (if your company downsized a lot then most likely the employees that are not with the company anymore are now Terminated in EC and fall off the licence count as a consequence as per example C in the first screenshot above).

 

Links

  • 3542507 - License Usage Report for Compensation Module

 

Thanks, 

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Create ALP with Chart and RAP V4 Service
entry.content:
Hi OsciPosci,

I came across this post looking for the exact same thing. After several days of investigating it against another RAP system, I also couldn't get an OData V4 ALP working. I'm posting this because your question has been unanswered for two months, and it's worth highlighting that this isn't an isolated case.

What I noticed: with another CDS (only with annotations @ui.chart, @ui.lineItem, @ui.selectionField, @Aggregation.default: #SUM), it works end-to-end with the FE V2 ALP template when exposed via OData V2 UI binding — table + chart + filter bar + KPIs, all native. The same CDS model, exposed via OData V4 UI binding, produces metadata that the ALP V4 wizard rejects with "must contain aggregate-based entities".

The issue seems to be in the combination of V4 binding and the V4 ALP wizard. What CAP emits and satisfies the wizard, the RAP V4 runtime doesn't emit in the same way.

If anyone from the community or SAP has managed to get ALP V4 working against RAP (not CAP) on S/4HANA on-premise, it would be really helpful to share a pointer or a sample. Also curious if there's a minimum Frontend Server / SAP_UI version where this is expected to work, since most of the documentation I've found is CAP-based. Any guidance is appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Creating Statutory Reports for Non-Supported Countrie...
entry.content:
Hello, great question. I assume you are familiar with the offering by SAP today:

S/4 Public Cloud Help Page for DRC: https://help.sap.com/viewer/71af4585db6d4904b1724730f3776c9b/LATEST/en-US/097165e9c1074038847625e5c53e07d2.html

Extensibility is on the roadmap for statutory reports not offered by SAP. Here some helpful resources:

  1. https://app.main.regulatory-change-manager.cloud.sap/index.html - Regulatory Change Manager
  2.  https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&BC=6EAE8B27FCC11ED892E91F270C7200CC#Q2%202025 – Roadmap Explorer
  3. https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/document-reporting-compliance - DRC Community

If you are open - there are partners already extending reports for countries today. I do not have any specifics countries and enablement in S/4 Public Cloud but contacting the partners that offer DRC scenarios in the SAP Store could be a first step, chances are some reports may already exist.

While I did not provide specific answers, this information may be helpful.

Best regards,

Martin

entry.title: You are the Voice of HR! Participate Now in Sapient Insights Group’s 29th Annual HR Systems Survey
entry.content:
The 29th Annual HR Systems Survey, conducted by Sapient Insights Group, is now open for participation through June 25th, 2025.  Your voice is the future of HR Technology!

 

TAKE THE SURVEY

This survey is valuable for anyone supporting their organization's HR systems and practices, interested in sharing their vendor experiences, and giving back to the HR community.

All responses are confidential and only used in aggregate results.

In 2025, over 4,600 organizations across the globe participated!

As a Thank You for your participation, you will receive the following:

  • Continuing education – Participants can earn up to 11 HRIP PDUs
    (Professional Development Units) - 10 for the survey + 1 more for the IHRIM member-exclusive webinar on December 16, 2026
  • A personalized data snapshot filtered by Industry or Region – your pick!
  • A full copy of the 29th Annual Sapient Insights 2026-2027 HR Systems Survey Report (350+ pages), an $1800 value, to be released in the Fall of 2026.

 

The first 100 respondents who complete the minimum survey requirements will also receive a $10 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation, and our 29th, 129th, 529th, 1029th, and 2029th respondents will be awarded a $100 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation.

The 29th Edition of the Annual HR Systems Survey includes these exciting updates:

The first 100 respondents who complete the minimum survey requirements will also receive a $10 gift card of their choice or option to make a $10 charitable donation, and our 29th, 129th, 529th, 1029th, and 2029th respondents will be awarded a $100 gift card or option to make a $100 charitable donation.

The 29th Edition of the Annual HR Systems Survey includes:

  • Extensive questions on the use of AI / Machine Learning throughout the survey
  • More options to collaborate with colleagues to complete the survey
  • A dynamic table of contents that allows you to easily navigate through the survey, making the best use of your time and expertise
  • Easily stored previous answers, so there is no pressure to complete the survey in one sitting
  • A 24-hour response time to any technical issues or survey questions
  • A YouTube video on “How to take the survey”

Survey topics include:

  • HR strategy, process, and structure
  • HR technology adoption in all applications
  • HR practices in Benefits, Talent Management, and Workforce planning
  • HRIS governance & organization structure
  • Functional & HR Tech support services
  • Deployment roadmaps, resourcing, and budgeting
  • Implementation, maintenance, and integration of HR Tech platforms
  • Change management and adaptive leadership practices
  • Emerging and innovative technology and trends

New section this year on finance, travel expense reporting, and financial planning

*The survey takes approximately 15-60+ minutes of your time and may be forwarded to other team members for additional feedback and knowledge sharing.

If you would like to preview the survey or if you have any additional questions before taking the survey, please contact Tammy Smith with the Sapient Insights Research team at research@sapientinsights.com.       

The survey results will debut at the HR Technology Conference October 20-22nd 2026. Our team looks forward to seeing you there! If you would like to purchase a copy of our 2025-2026 Annual HR Systems Report, you can do so here.

Thank you for sharing your voice and supporting this amazing global research community!

entry.title: How to define "PEER" Rater Category/Role in SuccessFactors 360 Reviews
entry.content:
I am new to SuccessFactors 360 Reviews, and I am learning to create a new 360 Review form with the workflow/process as mentioned below.

The form should be supported by a route map that allows the Employee to specify the participants. This selection will subsequently be reviewed and approved by the employee's manager, after which the evaluation will be forwarded to multiple raters, including Direct Reports, Peers, and/or the Manager. Upon completion of the evaluation, the confidentiality of the participants' feedback must be preserved from the employee, although it may be accessible to the manager and administrators. Attached are snapshots of the roles that are currently displayed under the form and the route map.

1.) How can I define the role category of a "peer," which could encompass anyone within the organization but outside the employee's direct hierarchy? For instance, this could include an EMM's counterpart or a colleague from the operations team, among others. Additionally, once the nomination of participants is approved, how does the Evaluation step incorporate these nominations to facilitate the feedback request?

2.) How are external participants defined ?

3.) How is such a role delineated within the Evaluation step of the route map?

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality
entry.content:
SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality 

With the new release V3.0, SAP Mobile Start got rebranded as the Joule Work mobile app, with enhanced Joule integration and advanced single sign-on functionality. 

Rebranding: SAP Mobile Start has been rebranded as the Joule Work mobile app, accompanied by a new app icon and an updated user experience. For customers previously accessing Joule within SAP Mobile Start, the Joule entry point has transitioned from the diamond in the header bar to a full-screen interface that can be accessed via the tab bar. This provides users faster access to Joule.  

All core functionality and features remain unchanged, ensuring continuity for both customers and end users with no disruption to existing workflows.

 Screenshot: Direct Joule access in tab bar

For customers running the Joule Work mobile app within their SAP Build Work Zone environment, existing theming and custom branding capabilities remain unchanged. Customers can continue to apply their current branding configurations without impact.

Enhanced Joule capabilities: With the latest release of the app, Joule Work mobile allows users to dictate their prompts to Joule to allow a fast entry of prompts and a better hands-free experience.

Screenshot: Joule dictation

Single–sign-on:

Currently, users can enjoy a Single Sign‑On–like experience for web content by using the “Remind Me” functionality provided by SAP Cloud Identity Services. With the Joule Work mobile app version 3.0, the app offers an advanced Single Sign-On (SSO) capability, reducing the need for users to manually log in when navigating to trusted web applications in the in-app browser. We plan to release a dedicated blog post explaining the feature in depth shortly.

I hope you will enjoy these new features in the Joule Work mobile app.. 

SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app is here!
entry.content:
So, what is Joule Work? Joule Work provides an AI-based user experience that puts the user into the center; and transforms how users work. With Joule Work, users can express what they want to accomplish, and Joule surfaces the right insights, automates routine work, and coordinates AI agents across systems to achieve it – all in one dynamic workspace, on the desktop as well as on mobile. Joule Work acts as an engagement layer that spans across SAP products and beyond and coexists with current experiences provided by pre-defined application UIs. 

Our first version of the Joule Work mobile app comes with two main elements: Conversations and Space.
Let have a look the at the two.

Conversations are AI powered chats with role-based intents, conversation threads, and dynamic response types. Conversations are the main way to interact with the system, users can articulate their needs in a certain context, and Joule will take the required actions or provide the right answers.
The Joule Work mobile app also allows users to have hands-free voice conversations with Joule what is very helpful while being on the go. 

 

 

Screenshot: Conversations, Conversation with context and voice mode

Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs.​ With the Joule Work mobile app, users can work with their spaces while being away from their desk.

Screenshot: Spaces

 

Please also check out our Joule Mobile sap.com page.  

Many of you might wonder, how does the new Joule Work mobile app relate to SAP Mobile Start. The Joule Work mobile app should be seen as an evolution of SAP Mobile Start. It will support both scenarios in the future.

It supports customers who want to explore the new Joule Work offering, as described in this blog with spaces and conversations. For customers running the Joule Work mobile app within their SAP Build Work Zone environment, all core functionality and features remain unchanged, ensuring continuity for both customers and end users with no disruption to existing workflows. Please also see my blog SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality.

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: [Professional Services] SAP Learning Curriculum for Professional Services
entry.content:
SAP Learning Curriculum for Professional Services

The Professional Services industry is evolving rapidly – and so is the way we learn SAP solutions for project-based businesses.

To better support learners, SAP provides updated Professional Services learning journeys and courses, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and modern service delivery models.

This blog helps you understand the key learning offerings available today and how to navigate your learning journey with clarity and confidence.

Overview of Key Professional Services Courses

Below is an overview of the core Professional Services learning content, based on the latest offerings on learning.sap.com.

0. ISR010 – Introduction to Professional Services in SAP

Industry-level introduction and business context

  • Provides a high-level overview of the Professional Services industry and its key characteristics
  • Introduces how SAP supports project-based and service-driven business models
  • Helps learners understand the end-to-end value chain from bid to bill

Designed for:

  • Beginners exploring the industry
  • Learners needing business context before diving into S/4HANA
  • Non-technical roles

➡️Positioned as the recommended course to understand the basics of the industry

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-the-professional-services-industry

1. ISR20 – Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Entry-level introduction to Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Focuses on SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s role in project delivery
  • Introduces key components such as:
    • Project management
    • Finance
    • Workforce management
  • Provides foundational understanding for working with SAP Professional Services solutions

Designed for:

  • Beginners
  • Consultants new to Professional Services

➡️Typically the starting point for the learning journey

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-fundamentals-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

2. ISR21 – Managing Projects and Resources in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Core course on project execution and resource management

  • Focuses on managing projects and resource allocation in Professional Services
  • Covers key activities such as:
    • Creating and managing resource requests
    • Understanding staffing processes
    • Matching resources to project requirements
  • Introduces time recording concepts, including:
    • Tracking work hours
    • Using SAP Fiori applications
    • Understanding role-based responsibilities

Designed for:

  • Project managers
  • Resource planners
  • Functional consultants

➡️Builds understanding of how to execute and manage projects effectively

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

3. ISR22 – Exploring Procurement Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Introduction to procurement processes supporting project delivery

  • Focuses on procurement of services and goods for project execution
  • Covers key topics such as:
    • Service procurement and goods purchasing
    • Third-party order processing
    • Supplier-related processes
  • Introduces core procurement elements including:
    • Requisitions
    • Supplier invoices
    • Contract creation

Designed for:

  • Consultants involved in sourcing and procurement
  • Project roles managing external resources or suppliers

➡️Explains how procurement processes integrate into Professional Services

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-procurement-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

4. ISR23 – Exploring Sales & Intercompany Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Focus on sales, billing, and revenue processes

  • Covers end-to-end sales order processing in project-based scenarios
  • Includes key topics such as:
    • Sales orders and billing documents
    • Credit and debit memo management
    • Event-Based Revenue Recognition
  • Introduces intercompany processes for service delivery

Designed for:

  • Consultants working on billing and revenue processes
  • Project roles responsible for financial execution

➡️Provides understanding of how services are billed and revenue is recognized

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

 

5. ISR30 – Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Implementation-focused course with hands-on scenarios

  • Focuses on implementing Professional Services solutions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Covers key areas such as:
    • HR connectivity
    • Project management
    • Sales order processing
    • Project billing
    • Intercompany processes
  • Includes practical learning elements:
    • Configuration lessons
    • System simulations
    • Hands-on exercises

Designed for:

  • Implementation consultants
  • Project team members preparing for real-world deployments

➡️Prepares learners for end-to-end implementation and practical project work

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

 

Learning Journey – Exploring End‑to‑End Implementation & Operations SAP Cloud ERP, Professional Services 

Structured, role-based learning path

The main Learning Journey:

👉Exploring End-to-End Implementation & Operations SAP Cloud ERP, Professional Services

  • Guides learners through the complete Professional Services value chain
  • Covers:
    • Core fundamentals and project execution
    • Staffing and resource management
    • Time recording
    • Service procurement and supplier processes
    • Sales order processing and billing
    • Event-Based Revenue Recognition
  • Includes:
    • Real-world scenarios
    • Configuration lessons
    • Simulations and exercises
  • Leads to certification readiness

➡️Serves as the central, structured learning path for Professional Services

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-end-to-end-implementation-operations-sap-cloud-erp-professional-services

What’s Changing in Professional Services Learning?

SAP learning for Professional Services has evolved to reflect:

  • Modern SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions
  • Strong focus on end-to-end business processes
  • Integration across:
    • Project delivery
    • Finance
    • Resource management

Key shift:

  • From isolated training topics
  • To structured learning journeys and role-based learning

Why These Courses Matter

The current curriculum helps you:

  • Understand how SAP supports project-based business models
  • Learn end-to-end processes instead of isolated features
  • Build skills aligned with:
    • Real project scenarios
    • Implementation and consulting roles

Professional Services solutions from SAP enable companies to:

  • Manage services more effectively
  • Optimize resources and workforce
  • Support XaaS-based business models
  • Analyze data across the service lifecycle

Recommended Learning Path

A typical progression:

  1. ISR010 – understand the basics of the industry
  2. ISR20 – Fundamentals. The starting point for the learning journey
  3. ISR21 – How to execute and manage projects effectively
  4. ISR22 – How procurement processes integrate into Professional Services
  5. ISR23 – How services are billed and revenue is recognized
  6. ISR30 – End-to-end implementation and practical project work
  7. Learning Journey – End-to-end structured learning

You Don’t Have to Do This Alone

To support your learning journey, the Industries Learning Group provides a dedicated place to:

  • Exchange with other learners
  • Ask questions:
    • Where should I start?
    • Which courses fit my role?
    • What should I learn next?
  • Share feedback on learning content

👉The goal is simple:
Help each other navigate SAP industry learning more easily

Get Started

If you are:

  • Starting your Professional Services learning journey
  • Preparing for SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects
  • Looking for structured enablement

👉Begin with ISR010, ISR20 or the Learning Journey
👉Follow the structured curriculum
👉Build your expertise step by step

Let’s learn SAP for Professional Services – together.

@Grzegorz

entry.title: How to obtain a OAuth2 refresh token using OA2C_CONFIG
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am setting up a Concur integration in SAP using transaction OA2C_CONFIG . I entered the Client ID and Client Secret and Access Settings as follows:

 

Since I was provided with a Company UUID and a Request Token by the Concur administrator, I am using the "Ressource Owner Password Credentials" grant type. But when I request the refresh token in the OA2C_CONFIG with the Company UUID and Request Token, I get a 403 error:

 

The  credentials are correct, since I can get the refresh token via Postman. The SSL certificates have been imported and firewall configurations are fine as well.

My plan is to use the OAuth2 Client with CL_OAUTH2_CLIENT to authorize the http client (cl_http_client ) and call the Concur APIs. 

Is this the correct way handling with with Company UUID and the OAuth2 Client?
Is there a better or recommended approach for this scenario?

Enviroment: SAP S/4HANA 2025
